scenario_description: A small potted cactus moves from the right side of a desk to the floor, then to the left side of the desk, and is finally covered by a white tray. A similar but larger cactus remains on a shelf in the background throughout.

turns.text: Here's my messy desk. I need to clean it up.
turns.image_description: A cluttered wooden desk surface. On the far right edge sits a small, round green cactus in a terracotta pot (Entity A). In the background, slightly out of focus on a high shelf, sits a much taller, columnar cactus in a blue ceramic pot (Distractor B). Various papers and a laptop are scattered around.

turns.text: Looks like a lot of work! That little guy on the right might get knocked over if you aren't careful moving things around.
Okay, I moved that one down so I could wipe the table.
turns.image_description: The same wooden desk is now completely clear of all items. The small round cactus in the terracotta pot (Entity A) is no longer visible on the desk; the space where it was is empty. The tall columnar cactus in the blue pot (Distractor B) is still visible on the high shelf in the background.

turns.text: Nice, the surface looks spotless now. Where did you put the little cactus?
Just put it back up here, but on the other side this time.
turns.image_description: The wooden desk is mostly clear again. The small round cactus in the terracotta pot (Entity A) is now positioned on the far left edge of the desk. The tall columnar cactus in the blue pot (Distractor B) remains on the high shelf in the background.

turns.text: Got it. So it's on the left now? Looks stable there.
Yeah, but I need to hide it for the photo shoot. Covered it with this tray.
turns.image_description: The wooden desk has a large, rectangular white paper tray placed on the left side. Underneath the tray, the outline of the small round cactus in the terracotta pot (Entity A) is faintly visible, suggesting it is directly beneath. The tall columnar cactus in the blue pot (Distractor B) is still clearly visible on the high shelf in the background, untouched.

turns.text: Ah, clever way to keep it out of the frame while keeping it nearby.
In the last photo, which plant is hidden under the white tray: A) The tall cactus on the shelf, B) The small cactus that started on the right, or C) The small cactus that started on the left?

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: 1. Turn 1 establishes the 'small cactus' (Entity A) starts on the right edge of the desk. 2. Turn 3 shows the desk cleared, implying Entity A was moved off-desk (to the floor per scenario logic, though not explicitly stated in text, its absence is key). 3. Turn 5 shows Entity A returned to the desk but on the LEFT side. 4. Turn 7 shows a white tray covering the object on the LEFT side. 5. The question asks what is under the tray. Since Entity A moved from Right -> Off -> Left -> Under Tray, the correct reference is 'The small cactus that started on the right'. Option A is wrong because the tall cactus never moved from the shelf. Option C is wrong because there was never a 'small cactus that started on the left'; the one currently on the left is the one that originated on the right.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must track the specific identity of the small cactus across multiple spatial changes (Right -> Off -> Left) to resolve the anaphoric reference 'that started on the right' against the distractor 'that started on the left' (which implies a different object that never existed) and the stationary tall cactus.

_meta.scenario: A small potted cactus is visible on the right edge of the desk. Over time, the user clears the entire desk surface, moving the cactus to the floor, then back onto the desk but to the left side, and finally covering it with a large white paper tray.
